pages.title: Under the Elevated
pages.tagline: A dark NYC R&B journey through addiction's seductive lie.

pages.body: Under the Elevated is a slow-burning cinematic exploration where late-night New York becomes a character itself. The track blends boom-bap drum patterns with dusty vinyl crackle and subway rumble, supporting vocals that shift from smooth vulnerability to desperate power. Here, success masks internal decay while addiction whispers seductively in the shadows of Queens Boulevard. As the arrangement rises through noir strings and raw piano, we confront the rationalization that keeps us trapped between paying bills and facing our demons. This is not just a song; it is an intimate confession found under the tracks where nobody checks on you.
pages.styleSentence: A slow-burning (83 BPM) dark NYC R&B track featuring a bass-heavy boom-bap drum pattern without modern 808s, layered with dusty vinyl crackle and subway rumble FX; the arrangement blends cinematic noir strings (cello, viola), smoky pads, deep live kicks, rimshots, and raw piano to support a smooth yet wounded male vocal that shifts from soft vulnerability to desperate power.

pages.text:
[Intro - Narrator, subway rumble]
This is for the ones nobody checks on
because they look like they got it together.
Bills paid. Kids fed. Smile ready.
But somewhere between Queens and Brooklyn,
under the elevated,
somebody is asking:
“Is this really addiction?”

[Female - whisper]
You work hard.
You deserve peace.
Come here...

[Verse 1 - Male, smooth R&B]
Queens Boulevard got my promises,
Brooklyn nights got my shame.
I keep showing up like I’m solid,
but I keep coming home the same.

Rent paid, lights on, fridge full,
everybody thinks I’m blessed.
I keep my body right, keep my money good,
still got smoke inside my chest.

Corner store glowing like a chapel,
midnight glass behind the door.
I tell myself I’m only looking,
but my hand already knows.

[Female - sung whisper]
Long day.
Don’t make it deep.
One little flame.
One little drink.
One little sleep.

[Pre-Chorus - Male]
And it don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like I’m still in control.
Like I could stop when I’m ready,
like I could leave it alone.

I quit three days just to prove it,
then came back like I won.
But that ain’t freedom,
that’s just losing with a different kind of gun.

[Narrator]
The cage don’t always rattle.
Sometimes it sings.

[Chorus - Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.
First time flew, now I’m stuck here waiting
with my heart face down on the floor.

It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

I wanna quit,
but I want that fire.
I want the truth,
but I love the liar.

And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Verse 2 - Male, rhythmic R&B]
On the J train with my jaw tight,
on the 7 with my head low.
Every ad says buy your freedom,
every craving says don’t let go.

I got side dreams, side gigs,
plans folded in my notes.
But I keep feeding little habits
that keep standing on my throat.

I say “next week” like a prayer,
say “last time” like a song.
Say “I’m good” when I’m lying,
say “I’m fine” when I’m gone.

[Female - addiction POV]
Gone?
Baby, you’re here.
I’m the only thing clear
when the city gets heavy.
I disappear your fear.

You call me poison,
but I call you mine.
You call it weakness,
I call it time.

[Male]
Don’t call me yours.

[Female]
Then why do you answer?

[Narrator]
Addiction don’t always kick the door in.
Sometimes it uses your own voice.
Sometimes it says,
“You earned this.”

[Pre-Chorus 2 - Male]
At the counter with the music low,
card in my hand, heart in my throat.
I ask myself, “Do I need this?”
then laugh like it’s some kind of joke.

What else could I do with this money?
What else could I do with this pain?
What man keeps building a future
then burning the bridge in the rain?

[Chorus - Duet]
[Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.

[Female]
I can make it feel close.
I can open that door.

[Male]
It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

[Female]
Borrow it, baby.
You can pay it later.

[Both]
And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Bridge - Narrator]
To the one saying “I can stop”
but never really stops:
this is the sign.
Not shame.
Not judgment.
Come back home.

[Bridge - Male]
Do I really need this?
Do I really want this?
Am I really free
if I keep running from the honest?

Do I want to stop,
or do I want to hide?
Do I want my life back,
or another alibi?

[Female - soft, dangerous]
Nobody knows you like I know you.
Nobody holds you like I hold you.

[Male - breaking]
That’s not love.
That’s not peace.
That’s you taking little pieces out of me.

I don’t need to hit bottom
just to prove the bottom’s real.
I don’t need another bottle
just to tell me how to feel.

[Breakdown - Male]
Help me.
Hear me.
I’m still in here.
Don’t fear me.

I look alright,
but I’m not okay.
I keep surviving,
but I wanna change.

Call me.
Hold me.
Don’t shame me.
Just know me.

I need love.
I need truth.
I need somebody
to pull me through.

[Female - tempting]
One more night.
One more flame.
One more pour.

[Final Chorus - Male]
I’m not chasing the dragon under the elevated,
not tonight, not like before.
First time’s gone and I’m done here waiting
for a ghost to give me more.

It did feel like addiction
when I finally saw the chain.
It did feel like a prison
when I called it by its name.

I wanna live
more than I want that fire.
I want the truth
more than I want the liar.

And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Final Duet]
[Female]
You’ll miss me by morning.

[Male]
Maybe I will.

[Female]
You’ll call me when it hurts.

[Male]
I’ll call someone real.

[Female]
You’ll come back to me.

[Male]
Not this time.

[Outro - Narrator]
For anybody fighting quiet:
you are not the thing you’re fighting.
Cry out.
Reach out.
Come back.
The night is loud,
but you are louder.

[Male - whisper]
Help me.
I’m ready.

[Female - fading]
One more...

[Male]
No.

pages.vocalStyle: Smooth vulnerable male R&B with melodic hip-hop phrasing; female whisper-singing representing addiction (seductive, gothic); spoken word narrator.

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Sings wounded truth with smooth, melodic phrasing that cuts deep.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Whispers seductive lies from the shadows to tempt the heart away.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps a bass-heavy boom-bap pulse without modern synthetic kicks.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Grooves low and heavy, anchoring the mood in dark NYC streets.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Breathes smoky pads that fill the silence between desperate moments.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Layers textures of vinyl crackle and subway rumble for atmosphere.

members.name: GUITAR
members.bio: Plays raw notes to support the piano's mournful, lonely melody.

members.name: KEYS
members.bio: Chords swell with cinematic weight behind every vulnerable confession.

members.name: STRINGS
members.bio: Cello and viola weave a noir soundtrack through the city night.
